Option Name: One hand — no shoulder stock
Description: A firearm held and operated with one or both hands on the grip; no shoulder stock is used.

Option Name: Two hands — braced against the shoulder
Description: A firearm requiring both hands in separate positions and typically braced against the shoulder with a buttstock.

Option Name: Rifled bore
Description: A long gun with a rifled barrel.

Option Name: No — magazine-fed with sliding action
Description: A pistol that automatically chambers the next round after firing using a sliding action.

A handgun — but not a self-loading pistol or revolver

Description: A self-loading long gun chambered for pistol-calibre ammunition; typically compact.

Description: A fully automatic long gun designed to be carried and operated by an individual or small crew.
